g.text: Trace: "Merchant creates payment of Rs 500. User selects UPI. Gateway authorizes via Razorpay, captures payment, notifies merchant via webhook."
STEP 2 - Payment is the central object.
— can't authorize twice. States enforce rules."
is pluggable. All implement same interface."
STEP 5 - Route to the right provider.
"Multiple providers. Which one for this payment?"
: try primary, if down use fallback."
payments. If same idempotency key is sent
twice, returns the original Payment instead
of creating a new one. Critical for retries."
POSTs to merchant, LedgerObserver records capture,
Payment: amount, currency, method, status, provider ref
Created -> Authorized -> Captured / Cancelled / Failed / Refunded
Razorpay / Stripe / Paytm — all implement PaymentProvider
Processor orchestrates: select provider -> call -> retry -> update state
Webhook, Ledger, AuditLog, FraudCheck all notified
Merchant auth, idempotency key prevents duplicate payments
